Class TemperatureService

java.lang.Object
ntnu.no.fs_v26.service.TemperatureService
@Service public class TemperatureService extends Object
Service class for temperature logging and monitoring.

Handles recording and retrieval of temperature readings as part of the IK-Mat food safety compliance module. Temperatures are validated against the acceptable range configured per equipment, and a deviation is automatically created when a reading falls outside that range.

  • Constructor Details

    • TemperatureService

      public TemperatureService()

  • Method Details

    • logTemperature

      public TemperatureLog logTemperature(Long equipmentId, double value, User user)
      Records a new temperature reading for a specific piece of equipment.

      Validates that the equipment belongs to the user's organization and that the temperature value is within the physically plausible range of -50 to 100 °C. If the reading is outside the equipment's configured acceptable range, a deviation is automatically created.

      Parameters:
      equipmentId - the ID of the equipment being logged
      value - the recorded temperature in degrees Celsius
      user - the authenticated user recording the reading
      Returns:
      the saved TemperatureLog
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the equipment is not found, belongs to a different organization, or the temperature value is out of the valid input range

    • getLogsForOrganization

      public List<TemperatureLog> getLogsForOrganization(User user)
      Returns all temperature logs for the authenticated user's organization.
      Parameters:
      user - the authenticated user
      Returns:
      a list of all TemperatureLog entries for the organization

    • getLatestLogs

      public List<TemperatureLog> getLatestLogs(User user, int limit)
      Returns the most recent temperature logs for the organization, up to the given limit.
      Parameters:
      user - the authenticated user
      limit - the maximum number of entries to return
      Returns:
      a list of the latest TemperatureLog entries sorted by timestamp descending

    • getHistory

      public List<TemperatureLog> getHistory(User user, Long equipmentId, String fromDate, String toDate, String status)
      Returns a filtered list of temperature logs for the organization.

      Supports optional filtering by equipment ID, date range, and deviation status. Results are sorted by timestamp descending.

      Parameters:
      user - the authenticated user
      equipmentId - optional filter by equipment ID; null returns all equipment
      fromDate - optional start date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD); null or blank to skip
      toDate - optional end date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD); null or blank to skip
      status - optional filter: "OK" for normal readings, "DEVIATION" for out-of-range; null returns all
      Returns:
      a filtered and sorted list of TemperatureLog entries

    • getSummary

      public Map<String,Object> getSummary(User user)
      Returns a dashboard summary of today's temperature activity for the organization.

      Includes the total number of measurements recorded today and how many of those were flagged as deviations.

      Parameters:
      user - the authenticated user
      Returns:
      a map containing measurementsToday and deviationsToday

    • getLatestDeviations

      public List<TemperatureLog> getLatestDeviations(User user, int limit)
      Returns the most recent temperature deviation entries for the organization.
      Parameters:
      user - the authenticated user
      limit - the maximum number of entries to return
      Returns:
      a list of the latest TemperatureLog entries flagged as deviations
